rxn_class_summary: Visible-light photosensitized intramolecular [2+2] cycloaddition of N-allylcarboxamide-tethered indoles (and related heterocycles) to give cyclobutane-pyrrolidinone-fused tetracyclic scaffolds via dearomative energy transfer

key_intermediates.name: triplet photosensitizer (PS*) generated via ISC after hv absorption
triplet excited-state substrate 4* (diradical) formed by energy transfer from PS*
INT-A: 1,4-diradical from 5-exo-trig attack of C2 radical onto tethered olefin (favored pathway)
INT-B: 1,4-diradical from 7-endo-trig attack of C3 radical onto tethered olefin (disfavored)

electron_flow: Visible light excites the photosensitizer to its singlet then triplet state via ISC; triplet-triplet energy transfer (~60 kcal/mol) to the indole substrate 4 generates a triplet diradical 4*, which undergoes stepwise intramolecular C-C bond formation (preferentially 5-exo-trig at C2 to give INT-A) followed by radical-radical combination to close the cyclobutane and deliver tetracyclic 1.
rate_determining_step: Stepwise C-C bond formation from triplet diradical 4* via 5-exo-trig closure (lower activation energy than 7-endo-trig) is energetically favored per DFT
notes: DFT shows triplet-singlet (T1-S0) free energy gap of the substrate must be below the PS triplet energy (<60 kcal/mol) for productive energy transfer; C2-tethered carboxamide substrates qualify (delta-G 58.2-58.7 kcal/mol for indoles IV-VI; indenes VII/VIII and benzofuran IX also viable at 53.7-58.5 kcal/mol), whereas unsubstituted indole (65.0 kcal/mol) is unreactive. Alternative 7-endo-trig pathway via INT-B is calculated but disfavored relative to 5-exo-trig via INT-A.
